title: timaeosia cerastioides
intro: Timaeosia cerastioides, also known as the Mediterranean or Rock Whitlow-grass, is a small, perennial herbaceous plant native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which can help alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The medicinal properties of Timaeosia cerastioides have also been found to have wound-healing properties and may help reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. In herbal preparations, Timaeosia cerastioides is often combined with other herbs to create teas, infusions, or poultices for its medicinal benefits.
